title: Notice Stress Signals
summary: Recognize early physical or emotional signs that you are becoming stressed.
learnerPromise: You will be able to spot the early signs that stress is building in your body or mind.
whyItMatters: Catching stress early gives you a chance to respond before it becomes overwhelming. This can help you feel more in control of difficult moments.
realLifeUses: Your shoulders tighten during a busy school or work day and you choose to take a short walk
You notice you are snapping at people more than usual and realize you need a break
Your stomach feels tense before a presentation and you decide to breathe slowly for a minute
You feel scattered and tired after several busy days and recognize it is time to slow down
materialsNeeded: A piece of paper or a notes app
result: A short personal list of your most common stress signals.
youWillLearn: How to tell the difference between normal tiredness and stress
What your own body and mood tend to do when pressure rises
How to check in with yourself during a busy day
When a stressful feeling is temporary or sticks around too long
miniChallenge: Sit quietly for two minutes and ask yourself: 'What is my body telling me right now?' Write down one physical feeling and one emotional feeling you notice.
steps: Stop what you are doing for just one minute
Scan your body from head to toe and notice any tension, pain, or discomfort
Ask yourself what emotion you are feeling right now — there is no wrong answer
Write down at least one body signal and one mood signal you noticed
Think back to the last time you felt this way and what was happening then
completionCriteria: You wrote down at least one physical and one emotional stress signal
You can describe a situation where noticing this signal would help you
commonProblems: It feels strange or uncomfortable to focus on your feelings at first
You might think 'I am fine' even when your body is tense — this is normal
It can be hard to find a quiet minute during a busy day
tips: There is no right or wrong signal — yours might be headaches, irritability, tiredness, or something else
Set a gentle phone reminder once a day to do a quick body scan
If your stress feels constant or unmanageable, talking to a trusted adult or counselor is a strong and healthy step
helpBoundary: If stress feels constant, overwhelming, or lasts for several weeks, consider speaking with a counselor, trusted adult, or healthcare provider.
